question_text: A chocolate bar contains 2000 kJ of energy. How much energy does it contain in kilocalories (kcal)? (Hint: 1 kcal = 4.2 kJ)
explanation: To convert from kJ to kcal, you divide the energy in kJ by 4.2. So 2000 ÷ 4.2 = 476 kcal. This is why food labels often show energy in both units—they measure the same thing but in different scales.
